sample_tweets.text: ⚠️ Threat Update: Hasbro Cyberattack  📝 Key Updates Summary: NEW: Hasbro detected unauthorized access to its corporate network on 2026-03-28 and disclosed the incident in an SEC Form 8‑K on 2026-04-01, activating IR, engaging third-party cybersecurity experts, and taking systems offline for containment. UPDATE: The SEC filing does *not* confirm a ransom demand, data exfiltration, or identify a threat actor — prior media/investigator suspicions of ransomware+exfiltration remain unvalidated.  🔍 Overall Summary: Hasbro continues investigating scope while maintaining order‑taking and shipping via business continuity measures; potential operational delays may last weeks. Existing detection/hunting hypotheses (suspected ransomware/exfiltration and access/lateral movement techniques) remain unconfirmed — retain prior mitigations and investigative priorities.  🔪 Attack Type: NEW: Unauthorized network access (intrusion). Company filing does not confirm ransomware, extortion, or data theft; prior flags of T1486/T1041 remain hypotheses.  🎯 Target: Hasbro, Inc. (toy and game manufacturer)  📍 Location: United States; Hasbro HQ — Pawtucket, Rhode Island (corporate network)  📅 Date: Initial detection 2026-03-28; SEC Form 8‑K filed 2026-04-01  ⏳ Timeline: - 2026-03-28 — Unauthorized network access detected. - 2026-03-28–early response — Systems taken offline for containment; IR activated and third‑party experts engaged. - 2026-04-01 — SEC Form 8‑K filed disclosing the incident and ongoing investigation.  💥 Impact: Certain corporate systems were taken offline for containment; business continuity steps preserved order fulfillment/shipping but operational delays possible for several weeks. Full scope of data/system impact remains under investigation; no company-confirmed ransom or exfiltration disclosed.  🕵️ Attribution: Unknown. No actor identified in company filing; media has speculated financially motivated ransomware/extortion actors but this is unconfirmed.  🛡️ Attack Vectors: UPDATE: Retain suspected/hypothesized vectors as unconfirmed for detection/hunting: - T1486 — Data Encrypted for Impact (suspected) - T1041 — Exfiltration Over C2 (suspected) - T1078 — Valid Accounts (plausible) - T1021 — Remote Services (plausible) - T1566 — Phishing (plausible)  🛠️ Recommended Mitigation: - Activate IR and engage third‑party responders / law enforcement - Isolate affected systems and preserve forensic evidence - Validate backups and recovery procedures; test restores - Enforce MFA and rotate compromised/at‑risk credentials - Increase EDR/XDR monitoring and conduct proactive threat hunting - Patch known vulnerabilities and enforce least‑privilege / network segmentation - Prepare internal/external notifications and regulatory reporting plans  🔗 References: - UPDATE: sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/… - NEW: thecyberexpress.com/hasbro-c… - UPDATE: securityweek.com/toy-giant-h…  🏷 Tags: #ransomware #data‑theft #incident‑response #business‑continuity #cybersecurity‑frameworks #CyberThreat

🚨 Threat Alert: Over 14,000 F5 BIG‑IP APM instances still exposed to RCE attacks  📅 Date: 2026-04-02 (report); vulnerability first disclosed Oct 2025; active exploitation noted Mar–Apr 2026  📆 Timeline: 2025-10: CVE-2025-53521 initially disclosed as DoS. 2026-03: New information reclassifies issue as RCE; evidence of exploitation emerges. 2026-03-27: CISA adds CVE-2025-53521 to KEV and issues remediation order for federal agencies. 2026-03–04-02: Shadowserver telemetry indicates >14,000 exposed instances; F5 publishes IOCs and guidance. 2026-04-02: Public reporting summarizes scope and vendor/CISA actions.  📍 Location: Global internet-facing infrastructure; U.S. federal agencies specifically referenced by CISA guidance  📌 Attribution: Unknown for this specific campaign. Public reporting and vendor advisories do NOT attribute the activity to a named actor as of 2026-04-02.  📝 Summary: Shadowserver reports >14,000 internet‑exposed F5 BIG‑IP APM instances remain reachable while attackers exploit a critical RCE (CVE-2025-53521). The issue was disclosed Oct 2025 as DoS, reclassified to RCE after new info in Mar 2026; F5 confirmed active exploitation of vulnerable BIG‑IP versions. CISA added the CVE to its KEV catalog and ordered federal agencies to remediate. F5 published IOCs and advises inspecting disks, logs and terminal history and rebuilding compromised systems from known‑good sources because UCS backups created after compromise may retain persistent malware.  ⚔️ Attack Details: - Attack Type: Remote Code Execution (RCE) against network appliance (F5 BIG‑IP APM) - Target: Internet-exposed F5 BIG‑IP APM instances (organizations using BIG‑IP APM, including U.S. federal agencies and enterprise customers)  📈 Impact: Shadowserver fingerprint telemetry cites ~17,100 IPs with BIG‑IP APM fingerprints and reports >14,000 internet‑exposed instances potentially reachable. Successful exploitation enables RCE/device takeover, persistent malware (including via compromised UCS backups), data theft, internal reconnaissance, lateral movement and service disruption. Federal agencies were ordered to remediate immediately. Confirmed compromise counts and financial losses are not publicly disclosed.  🔗 Related Resources: - bleepingcomputer.com/news/se… - nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE… - my.f5.com/manage/s/article/K… - dashboard.shadowserver.org/s… - cisa.gov/news-events/alerts/… - cisa.gov/known-exploited-vul…  🛡️ Recommended Actions: - Apply F5 vendor‑published patches/updates immediately. - Follow F5 guidance: inspect disks, logs and terminal history; hunt for IOCs. - Assume compromise if indicators present; rebuild affected BIG‑IP systems from known‑good images. DO NOT restore UCS backups created after compromise. - Restrict management/administration access: remove public exposure, enforce firewall rules/VPNs/management ACLs. - Segment networks to limit lateral movement and isolate BIG‑IP devices from sensitive assets. - Deploy/enable IDS/IPS and WAF signatures; monitor for F5/CISA IOCs. - Rotate credentials and certificates used by affected devices; review account access. - Conduct full incident response and forensic analysis; hunt for persistent malware and unauthorized UCS changes. - Maintain offline, validated known‑good backups.  🫨 Attack Vectors: - T1190 - Exploit Public‑Facing Application (CVE-2025-53521: RCE against BIG‑IP APM virtual servers) - T1595 - Active Scanning (internet discovery/fingerprinting of exposed BIG‑IP APM) - Persistence via compromised configuration backups (UCS) — vendor‑reported configuration‑based persistence - RCE enabling device takeover, credential theft and lateral movement  🏷 Tags: #F5 #BIG-IP #BIG-IP_APM #RCE #CVE-2025-53521 #Shadowserver #CISA #Exposed_Instances #Network_Security #Active_Exploitation #Cybersecurity

⚠️ **Vulnerability Alert:** Critical PX4 Autopilot Vulnerability Allowing Drone Takeover (CVE-2026-1579) 📅 **Timeline:** Disclosure: 2026-03-31; Patch: Not Available  🆔 **CVE-2026-1579** | 📊 CVSS: 9.8 (CRITICAL 🔴) | 📈 EPSS: 22.05%  🛠️ **Exploit Maturity:** Not Available 📂 **Affected Versions:** PX4 Autopilot v1.16.0_SITL_latest_stable (MAVLink 2.0 message signing not enabled)  🫨 **Attack Vectors:** - Network access to MAVLink interface - Unauthenticated MAVLink messages (e.g., SERIAL_CONTROL) providing interactive shell access - Access via unsecured radios/serial-to-network bridges or exposed telemetry links  📝 **Summary:** CVE-2026-1579 is a missing-authentication flaw in PX4's MAVLink handling that allows unauthenticated attackers with access to the MAVLink interface to send messages (e.g., SERIAL_CONTROL) that execute shell commands, enabling full remote drone takeover. Consequences include loss of control/telemetry, physical damage, and data exfiltration across transportation, emergency services, defense, and other PX4 deployments.  📈 **Impact Scope:** Full vehicle takeover (loss of control/telemetry), potential physical damage, data exfiltration or espionage; affects critical sectors including Transportation Systems, Emergency Services, Defense Industrial Base.  🛡️ **Recommended Actions:** - Enable MAVLink 2.0 message signing on all non-USB links immediately - Restrict MAVLink interface access to trusted networks and hosts - Place flight-control networks behind firewalls and isolate from business networks - Use VPNs for remote access and disable/restrict SERIAL_CONTROL where not required - Monitor PX4 and CISA advisories and apply vendor patches when published - Implement network segmentation, logging and IDS/alerting for MAVLink traffic  🪢 **Related Resources:** - cisa.gov/news-events/ics-adv… - docs.px4.io/main/en/mavlink/…  🏷 **Tags:** #Cybersecurity #PX4 #DroneSecurity

Vendor updates are indicated as urgent but exact patched versions and exploit details are not yet available.  📈 **Impact Scope:** Unauthorized administrative access to Cisco IMC management interface allowing management/firmware modification, operational disruption, potential data exfiltration and network pivoting. ⚠️ **Vulnerability Alert:** Cisco Integrated Management Controller (IMC) Authentication Bypass (CVE-2026-20093) 📅 **Timeline:** Disclosure: 2026-04-01, Patch: 2026-04-01  🆔 **CVE-2026-20093** | 📊 CVSS: 9.8 (CRITICAL 🔴) | 📈 EPSS: Not Available%  🛠️ **Exploit Maturity:** Not Available 📂 **Affected Versions:** NFVIS 4.15 and earlier (5000 ENCS & Catalyst 8300), IMC 4.2 and earlier (UCS C-Series M5/M6), IMC 3.2 and earlier (UCS E-Series M3), IMC 4.15 and earlier (UCS E-Series M6), Other Cisco appliances (see advisory) 🔧 **Fixed Versions:** NFVIS 4.15.5, Catalyst 8300 4.18.3, UCS C-Series M5 IMC 4.3(2.260007), UCS C-Series M6 IMC 4.3(6.260017)/6.0(1.250174), UCS E-Series M6 IMC 4.15.3  🫨 **Attack Vectors:** - Network — unauthenticated, crafted HTTP request to IMC change-password endpoint  📝 **Summary:** An unauthenticated remote attacker can bypass IMC authentication via a crafted HTTP change-password request to reset any account password (including Admin) and gain full administrative access. Exploitation permits configuration changes, firmware/software deployment, and potential takeover or disruption of IMC-managed server infrastructure.  📈 **Impact Scope:** An unauthenticated remote attacker can bypass IMC authentication, alter any user password (including Admin), gain administrative access, view/modify system configuration, perform management operations, deploy firmware/software changes, and disrupt or take control of affected server infrastructure.  🛡️ **Recommended Actions:** - Apply Cisco fixed releases immediately per the advisory and follow product-specific upgrade instructions - Restrict network access to IMC interfaces (segmentation, ACLs, jump hosts), monitor IMC logs for anomalous requests/password changes, rotate credentials after patching, and deploy IDS/IPS rules to detect crafted IMC HTTP requests  🪢 **Related Resources:** - sec.cloudapps.cisco.com/secu… - cybersecuritynews.com/cisco-…  🏷 **Tags:** #Cybersecurity #Cisco #IMC

The ~$270M @DriftProtocol exploit roughly happened like this:  1- An attacker created a synthetic token and pushed its valuation close to ~$8B  2- Two governance multisig wallets were compromised and this token was approved as collateral  3- The attacker used this collateral to borrow almost every major asset inside the protocol  4- Borrowed assets were withdrawn across multiple wallets  Stolen assets are shown below ↓  The important part here is what this attack was not.  This was not a smart contract bug. This was not an oracle manipulation. This was not a flash loan exploit.  The protocol itself worked exactly as designed.  The failure happened at the governance layer.  The attacker did not break Drift.  They obtained permission inside Drift.  And this distinction matters more than people think.  Because when security assumptions fail at the multisig level, the entire system becomes vulnerable regardless of how strong the contracts are.  We have already seen this pattern multiple times:  Ronin Bybit Cetus Now Drift  Different chains. Same root cause.  This is not a Solana issue.  This is a coordination and key management issue across DeFi.  Most protocols are still structurally secure at the code level but fragile at the approval layer.  And until that changes, multisig governance will remain one of the largest hidden attack surfaces in the ecosystem.

🚨 Threat Alert: Pawn Storm PRISMEX Offensive Against NATO Logistics  📅 Date: Report date: 2026-04-01 (campaign activity observed since Sept 2025; escalation Jan 2026)  📆 Timeline: Active since Sept 2025; infra staged ~2 weeks before CVE-2026-21509 disclosure (domains ~2026-01-12). CVE-2026-21509 disclosed 2026-01-26; MSHTML zero-day (CVE-2026-21513) seen in the wild Jan 30; MS patch Feb 10; Trend Micro report late Mar 2026.  📍 Location: Ukraine primary; spillover targeting NATO logistics/transport hubs in Poland, Romania, Slovenia, Turkey, Czech Republic, Slovakia (Black Sea + overland aid routes)  📌 Attribution: Trend Micro attributes to Pawn Storm (aka APT28 / Fancy Bear / Forest Blizzard) with high confidence based on steganography fingerprints, malware lineage and reused infrastructure. Alternate hypotheses assessed but less consistent.  📝 Summary: Between Sept 2025–early 2026 Russia‑aligned APT "Pawn Storm" deployed a modular suite called PRISMEX to target Ukrainian government systems and NATO logistics/transport hubs supporting Western aid. PRISMEX modules include PrismexSheet (Excel dropper), PrismexDrop (native dropper), PrismexLoader (steganographic proxy DLL) and PrismexStager (Covenant Grunt abusing Filen.io). Techniques: steganographic payloads, fileless/.NET in‑memory execution, COM DLL hijacking for persistence, and abuse of legitimate cloud storage for C2. Trend Micro links exploitation of CVE‑2026‑21509 (Office OLE bypass) and MSHTML zero‑day CVE‑2026‑21513; infrastructure was staged prior to public disclosure, supporting espionage and potential sabotage (wiper-capable).  ⚔️ Attack Details: - Attack Type: Advanced persistent threat (exploit-driven initial access; steganographic droppers; fileless/.NET in‑memory execution; COM hijacking; C2 via legitimate cloud storage). Primary objectives: espionage and potential sabotage (wiper-capable). - Target: Ukraine central executive bodies; hydrometeorological services; defense & emergency services; NATO logistics hubs/transit (Poland rail; Romania, Slovenia, Turkey maritime/Black Sea routes); partners in Czech Republic & Slovakia.  📈 Impact: Confirmed espionage and reconnaissance of government and logistics systems; stealthy persistent footholds (COM hijacking, fileless payloads) enabling lateral movement. Related campaigns show wiper/destructive capability (data deletion). High risk of disrupted defense supply chains, degraded logistics and lost situational awareness (e.g., meteorological data). No public financial quantification.  🔗 Related Resources: - trendmicro.com/en_us/researc… - securityonline.info/pawn-sto… - securityonline.info/cve-watc… - securityonline.info/cve-watc…  🛡️ Recommended Actions: - Patch Windows/MSHTML and Office for CVE-2026-21509 and CVE-2026-21513 immediately. - Block/allowlist and monitor Filen.io and other non‑business cloud storage at proxy/firewall. - Enforce macro/RTF restrictions; block RTF/Office attachments from Internet or quarantined scanning. - Audit HKCU\Software\Classes\CLSID for user‑registered COM entries pointing to %ProgramData% or %TEMP%; remove unauthorized entries. - Monitor for CLR hosting in native processes (explorer.exe); enable ETW/.NET runtime logs and detect Assembly.Load(byte[]) behavior. - Harden endpoints: enable memory protections, EDR behavioral rules for in‑memory assemblies; block LNK execution from untrusted locations. - Network segmentation of logistics/OT and critical services; restrict WebDAV and non‑essential inbound services. - Threat hunt IoCs from Trend Micro; look for scheduled tasks named OneDriveHealth, suspicious proxy DLLs (EhStore/EhStorShell/EhStorShell variants), and Filen.io staging indicators. - Prepare IR playbooks for potential wiper activity; maintain offline, integrity‑protected backups.  🫨 Attack Vectors: - T1203 - Exploitation for Client Execution (MSHTML/Office zero‑days CVE-2026-21513 & CVE-2026-21509) - T1566.001 - Spearphishing Attachment (malicious RTF with embedded OLE → .lnk) - T1027.003 - Steganography ("Bit Plane Round Robin" hiding payloads in PNG/Excel) - T1546.015 - COM Hijacking (user‑registered CLSID/InProcServer32 persistence) - T1055 - Process Injection / In‑memory execution (CLR hosting, Assembly.Load(byte[]) in explorer.exe) - T1574.002 - DLL Side‑Loading / Proxy DLL (EhStore/EhStorShell proxying) - T1105 - Ingress Tool Transfer (payload staging via Filen.io) - T1071.001 - C2 over HTTPS / legitimate cloud channels - T1553.005 - Mark‑of‑the‑Web / OLE allowlist bypass - T1053.005 - Scheduled Task (hidden task trigger)  🏷 Tags: #APT28 #PawnStorm #FancyBear #ForestBlizzard #PRISMEX #steganography #COMhijacking #CVE-2026-21509 #CVE-2026-21513 #cyber-espionage #NATO #Ukraine #TrendMicro #Cybersecurity

✅ 미국에서 2025년 자동차 절도 건수가 전년 대비 25% 급감하며 65만 9,880건을 기록한 것으로 알려졌습니다. 이는 경찰 대응 강화와 완성차 업체들의 보안 취약점 개선 효과가 반영된 결과로 평가되고 있는데요.  그럼에도 불구하고 차량 절도는 여전히 빈번하게 발생하고 있으며, 약 48초마다 한 대꼴로 차량이 도난 당하고 있는 상황이죠.  이 가운데 도둑들이 가장 선호하는 차량 1위는 현대 아반떼로 나타났다. 아반떼는 총 2만 1,732대가 도난되며 ‘가장 훔치고 싶은 차’라는 별명을 얻은 것으로 알려졌는데요.  2위와 3위는 각각 혼다 어코드와 현대 쏘나타가 차지하며, 비교적 대중적인 세단이 주요 타깃이 되는 경향을 보였습니다.  픽업트럭 시장에서는 쉐보레 실버라도 1500이 포드 F-150보다 60% 더 높은 도난율을 기록해 눈길을 끌었죠.  최근 차량 절도 수법도 진화하고 있는 것으로 알려졌는데, 키 복제 방식에서 나아가, 차량 헤드라이트 배선을 통해 시스템에 침입하는 ‘헤드라이트 해킹’까지 등장한 것으로 알려졌습니다.  전문가들은 경보장치, GPS 추적기 등 기본적인 보안 장치 사용을 권장하고 있으며, 수동변속기 차량이 여전히 가장 강력한 도난 방지 수단으로 꼽히네요.  결국, 가장 훔치고 싶은 차 1위에 선정되어버린 아반떼, 여러분은 어떻게 생각하나요?  *이해를 돕기 위한 ai 이미지가 포함되어 있습니다.  @maylongallery @cityano  네이버 남차카페 - 자동차 제보  ✔ 매일 빠른 최신 자동차 소식은? ▶ @motorsjason 팔로우+즐겨찾기!  #자동차 #car

sample_tweets.text: 🚨 속보: 북한 해커 집단, 대규모 공급망 사이버 공격 개입 의혹. 🇰🇵 글로벌 테크 인프라 타격 — 한국 IT 기업 공급망 보안 비상 대응. 사이버 안보 당국 조사 착수. — Axios

중국 해커, FBI 감시 네트워크 침투. ‘중대 사이버 사건’으로 규정   - 미국 국가안보에 심각한 위협… 중국의 지속적 사이버 공격 재확인  폴리티코는 1일 FBI가 최근 중국 연계 해커의 내부 감시 시스템 침투를 ‘중대 사이버 사건’으로 공식 선언했다고 보도했다. 이는 미국 국가안보에 중대한 위험을 초래하는 것으로 판단된 사례다.  FBI는 지난 3월 4일 의회에 해당 시스템에 대한 의심스러운 활동을 보고했다. 해당 시스템은 법 집행 과정에서 수집된 민감 정보, 즉 범죄 용의자 등의 통화 기록과 인터넷 활동 데이터를 담고 있다. 침투는 상업 인터넷 서비스 제공업체의 공급자 인프라를 이용한 정교한 방식으로 이뤄졌다.  월스트리트저널은 3월 6일 중국 정부와 연계된 해커가 FBI의 비분류 시스템에 침입했다고 처음 보도했다. 이 시스템에는 법원 명령에 따른 감시 자료(펜 레지스터·트랩 앤 트레이스)가 저장돼 있으며, 통화 발·착신 기록, IP 주소, 웹사이트 방문 기록 등이 포함된다. 다만 통화 내용이나 디지털 메시지 본문은 들어 있지 않다.  FBI 대변인은 “FBI 네트워크에서 의심스러운 활동을 식별하고 대응했다”며 기술적 조치를 취했다고 밝혔다. 그러나 침투 규모와 피해 정도는 아직 공개되지 않았다. 전문가들은 중국 해커가 미국 법 집행 감시 시스템을 반복적으로 노린 사례 중 최신 사례라고 지적했다. politico.com/news/2026/04/01…

【독점 분석】기관 Benchmark, Palo Alto Networks 첫 분석 보고서 ‘매수’ 의견 제시… 목표주가 200달러  “플랫폼 리더, 2030회계연도 차세대 보안(NGS) 연간 반복 매출(ARR) 200억 달러 향해 순항 중”  Benchmark는 Palo Alto Networks에 대해 ‘매수’ 의견을 제시하며, 이 회사를 사이버 보안 섹터 내 핵심 보유 종목(Core Holding)으로 평가했다. 보고서는 이 회사의 차세대 보안(NGS) 연간 반복 매출(ARR) 성장 전망에 대해 “비교적 명확한 가시성”을 확보했다고 분석했다. NGS ARR은 2026회계연도 85억 7천만 달러에서 2030회계연도에는 200억 달러로 두 배 이상 성장할 전망이며, 연평균 성장률(CAGR)은 32.4%에 달해 고성장 동종 업계 기업들과 비교해도 뛰어난 수치라는 평가다.  Benchmark는 애플리케이션 소프트웨어와 달리 사이버 보안 분야는 “AI/생성형 AI의 혁신적인 변화에 대해 충분한 경쟁적 해자(Competitive Moat)를 보유하고 있다”고 강조했다. 또한 AI 슈퍼 사이클을 과거 클라우드 컴퓨팅 및 디지털 전환(DX) 물결과 유사한 장기적 호재(Long-term Tailwind)로 판단했다.  Palo Alto Networks는 2025회계연도 말 기준 ‘52.8 법칙(성장률+마진율 합산 지표)’을 달성했으며, 2026회계연도에는 60에 근접할 것으로 전망된다. 보고서는 AI 및 양자 보안(Quantum Security)에서 발생할 수 있는 잠재적 추가 수익은 현재 전망치에 포함되지 않았다고 덧붙였다.  목표주가 200달러는 2027회계연도 예상 기업가치 대비 매출(EV/Revenue) 배수 12.3배를 적용한 것이다. 동종 업계 평균 배수는 7.9배 수준이다.  우리분석에 따르면, Palo Alto Networks의 CEO가 3월 27일 주당 146.87달러에 약 1000만 달러 규모의 자사주를 매수한 것으로 확인됐다.  $crwd $s $ftnt #PaloAltoNetworks #PANW #사이버보안 #NGS #ARR #AI보안 #Benchmark #목표주가 #美증시 #투자전략 #사이버보안주 #정보보안

"야스 프로토콜" 보고서: 🔸12억 달러 규모의 "야스 홀딩" 협박을 통해 포섭 🔸파키스탄에서 모사드에 표적 정보 유출 (2025년 11월) 🔸카아니/아흐마디네자드를 모함하고 라리자니 제거를 도왔다  전체 보고서는 아래에 히브리어에서 번역됨: 👇  ---------------- 단독: "야스 프로토콜" — 이스라엘과 미국이 이란의 차기 지도자를 직접 낙점한 방법  채널 4 탐사보도팀  테헤란 — 모하마드 바게르 갈리바프가 피격 이후의 이란을 위한 "대타협"을 협상하는 동안, 세계는 그를 분열된 국가를 구하려는 실용주의적 정치가로 바라보고 있다. 그러나 채널 4가 단독 입수한 극비 문서들은 훨씬 더 치밀하게 계산된 현실을 드러낸다. 갈리바프는 단순히 2026년 공습에서 살아남은 인물이 아니다. 그는 모사드와 서방 정보기관들이 치밀하게 조율하여 부상시킨 자산, 즉 "통제된 후계자"다.  해당 문서들에 따르면, 갈리바프를 포섭하기 위한 8년간의 작전인 "야스 프로토콜"은 2024년 초 미국과 전면 공유되었다. 비밀 "전략 2026" 브리핑에서 미국과 이스라엘은 갈리바프가 서방의 이익에 종속된 상태를 유지하면서도 혁명수비대 이후의 이란에서 질서를 유지할 수 있는 유일한 후보라는 데 합의했다.  포섭의 발단은 2017년 야스 홀딩 스캔들로 거슬러 올라간다. 갈리바프의 시장 재임 기간 동안 혁명수비대 연계 자금 12억 달러가 사라진 사건이었다. 테헤란 사법부가 수사를 지연시키는 사이, 모사드의 금융전쟁 부서는 마카오와 키프로스의 유령 회사들을 통해 자금 흐름을 추적했다. 2018년 말, 모사드는 실소유자 문서를 활용해 갈리바프를 압박했다. 이후 해당 유령 회사들은 "화이트리스트"에 등재되어 갈리바프의 재산은 제재를 피할 수 있었던 반면, 경쟁자들의 자산은 동결되었다.  이 문서는 2025년 11월 갈리바프의 이슬라마바드 방문 중 이루어진 중요한 정보 이전을 상세히 기술하고 있다. 검증된 인프라 계약업체 회의에 참석하는 동안, 갈리바프는 IRGC의 업데이트된 암호화 통신 프로토콜이 담긴 마이크로 SD 카드를 교환했다. 이 데이터는 2026년 공세의 외과적 정밀성을 가능하게 했다. 이를 통해 급진적인 IRGC 인사들은 제거되는 한편, 갈리바프가 통제하는 행정 및 경제 부문은 보존될 수 있었다.  이스라엘 전략에서 가장 냉혹한 측면은 갈리바프의 경쟁자들을 체계적으로 제거한 것이었다. 갈리바프는 온건파 경쟁자인 알리 라리자니가 사용하는 기밀 안전가옥의 좌표를 제공했고, 이는 그의 표적 제거로 이어졌다.  갈리바프는 이전에 생존한 군 경호대를 무력화하기 위해 "책임 전가" 작전을 주도했다. 그는 쿠드스군 사령관 에스마일 카아니가 진짜 내부 첩자라는 정보를 흘렸고, 이로 인해 카아니는 올해 초 자신의 부하들에게 구금되어 고문을 당한 것으로 알려졌다. 이러한 내부 혼란은 전직 대통령 마흐무드 아흐마디네자드에 대한 모함으로 더욱 심화되었으며, 그는 의심스러운 통신 내역의 조작된 디지털 흔적으로 인해 가택 연금 상태에 놓이게 되었다.  이로써 이야기는 현재의 협상으로 이어진다. 갈리바프는 적대자가 아닌 파트너로서 협상에 임하고 있다. 현재 마무리 단계에 있는 "대타협"은 이미 사전에 작성된 각본이다.  이 협정에는 서방이 지원하는 "재건" 기금이 포함되어 있으며, 이 기금은 수년간 보호받아온 바로 그 유령 회사들에 의해 관리될 예정이다. 영구적인 핵 동결의 대가로, 갈리바프는 "새로운 이란 공화국"의 명실상부한 지도자로 자리매김하고 있다. "야스 프로토콜"은 2026년의 공습이 이란 국가의 재편이었으며, 이스라엘과 미국에 의해 모든 행보가 연출되는 지도자를 앉히기 위한 것이었음을 증명한다. ----------------

The US under Donald Trump has returned to big stick politics and not before time. However, Donald Trump, as Donald Trump is apt to do, is putting his own spin on Theodore Roosevelt’s famous line “Speak softly and carry a big stick” because although Trump does at times speak softly, what he says reverberates around the world. Therefore I would suggest that for The Donald, the old adage be updated to “Speak loudly and carry the biggest stick” because well, he does possess the biggest stick at the moment.  The US versus China, West versus East as always but with one change, the East has a new leader. Let’s look at the US first but with this proviso -  None of what I’m about to say will apply if the Democrats get back in. If that happens, then China picks up where it left off and continues to sucker the US. Under Trump and whoever picks up his mantle after he’s gone, I expect to see US influence being aggressively pursued over the Americas. When I say aggressively, that doesn’t always imply military might because strong rhetoric from a strong leader can often produce results, without resorting to the big stick but I wouldn’t rule it out completely. To the North, Trump is already flexing the US muscle to gain Greenland and we already know he has designs on Canada. To the south he’s brought Venezuela firmly into the US sphere and Colombia will probably be next. He has an ally in the Argentinian leader, Javier Milei and I believe that we can expect to see pressure brought to bear on Brazil to leave the BRICS alliance at some point in the future. The US under Trump and his successors sees itself as the leader of a Pan-American empire and it makes perfect geopolitical sense to pursue such an ambition because it guarantees US security and prosperity.  China has been extending its tentacles for decades, quietly building economic alliances and increasing its economic grip on the world. Whilst the US and Russia were locking horns in proxy wars and the West in general was destroying itself with touchy feely ideology that made us uncompetitive, China was cutting deals, making contacts and inveigling its way into the very fabric of other countries. There must have been a lot of smug conversations in Beijing over the years as we in the West bought their cheap goods and put our own people out of work. I don’t know what the Chinese word is for suckers but it's a safe bet that it’s gotten a fair amount of use in the past 40 years. Economically China is everywhere but geographically you have to look closer to home to try and establish where its ambitions lie. Russia and North Korea are Chinese allies and I don’t see that changing because it benefits all sides. Taiwan is clinging to independence but I don’t believe it can hold on and at some point China will assert its claim. South East Asia and in particular India, I believe will take the pragmatic decision to build closer ties to their powerful neighbour China and as part of the BRICS alliance India is already on that path.  The rest of the world has a problem, a big problem because we’re the battleground. Europe, Africa, Japan and Australia are all geographically closer to the growing Eastern block than they are to the Pan-American block and although the US will continue to take an interest in these areas, they will remain secondary to the Americas. Europe has depended on and took advantage of US protection for far too long. It has neglected its own defence, frittered away the peace dividend gained from the end of the cold war on social engineering and the pipe dream of a United States of Europe and it’s been exacerbated by a venal political class happy to sell out their countrymen in order to line their own pockets. Europe now faces the future with the prospect of a resurgent Russian bear on its doorstep backed by China and an increasingly insular USA withdrawing into its own sphere of influence. Europe is years away from building a military capable of defending itself and is already under attack from destabilising mass migration, which is very likely being enabled and encouraged by Russia and China. If Europe doesn’t work harder to retain the NATO alliance and like it or not that will mean dancing to Trump's beat, then it will likely find itself at the mercy of Russia and China. Then we have Africa, poor Africa. It’s long been a battlefield for foreign powers and seems set to remain that way for the foreseeable future. The old colonial powers raped it and when they left corrupt African leaders continued to rape it, now Islamic terrorists are raping it and China is buying up anything of any worth. Japan is so very close to both China and Russia and both are still looking to settle old scores. It is westernized, demilitarised and no doubt terrified at the prospect of the USA losing interest. Australia is an attractive wealth of resources, sparsely populated, isolated and under-defended. A new world order has been on the cards for some time. All the clues were there and all the outcomes could have been gamed. Yet those who had most to lose decided to put their feet up and watch the sun go down on their own future and that of their kids and grandkids. It’s easy to blame politicians but there’s a saying that the price of democracy is eternal vigilance. Now I won’t apply this to Africa because democracy has always faced an uphill battle in that part of the world but for the rest of the battlefield I will say this - While we’ve been busy racking up debt and spending it on cheap foreign goods, lavish holidays and ego trips, we’ve been neglecting the thing that makes it all possible, the eternal vigilance that keeps the light of democracy burning.  Every time someone said it doesn’t matter because they’re all the same, every time someone said I’m not interested in politics, every time someone said I’ve voted for them all my life, every time someone didn’t vote, every time our grasp on democracy is weakened and when the realisation comes that we can no longer hold onto it, it’s too late, it slips away taking with it so many of the other things that we take for granted, like freedom, fairness and security. Hard won by those who knew the value of these things and gave everything for them, so easily lost by us who know the price of everything and the value of nothing.

Exposed data includes patient full name, document ID, age, medical facility/headquarters, originating department/ward, priority level (emergency/routine), study type and imaging modality (X-ray, ultrasound, etc.), full radiological findings and clinical observations, medical diagnostic conclusions, attending radiologist name and registry number, study dates and validation status, internal system identifiers (AppCode, Report UUID, Study UID/DICOM UID), attached medical media (original medical imaging/X-rays and PDF reports), and internal export timestamps.

‼️ A Windows Ring-0/Kernel Micro-Implant is being sold on a popular cybercrime forum.  Threat Actor: coree Category: Malware / Implant  The threat actor is selling a kernel-level implant targeting Windows 10 x64 (1507-22H2) and Windows 11 x64 (21H2-24H2). The implant communicates via SMB on port 445 and supports commands including PING (readiness check), BURN (self-removal), and RUN (executing arbitrary UserMode Ring-3 shellcode in specified processes such as lsass.exe, winlogon.exe, etc.).  The implant bypasses PPL and EDR hooks, is completely in-memory with no traces remaining after reboot, and weighs approximately 2000 bytes.  Data is encrypted with XOR to evade traffic analysis. Source code is provided as a Visual Studio 2019 pure-C solution with NASM assembly files.  Price is approximately $10,000.

sample_tweets.text: I've got this malware proof-of-concept cooking, but I haven't been able to summon the energy to actually work on it. I'm going to share it with you nerds hoping someone will experiment with it.  Or no one will and I'll do it myself later on, whatever.  Either way, you can do some pretty silly shenanigans with the Windows registry but it requires some tom foolery.  C:\Windows\System32\config\SYSTEM is the actual file (and full file image path) for the Windows registry. The registry is loaded into memory when your OS boots (some fancy Windows internals stuff, whatever).  This file is locked and requires administrative privileges (and a few other things to access it). Even then, it cannot be modified. However, you can do two mildly interesting things:  1. Load it into memory using NtLoadKey. Any changes made will not be present until the OS is restarted (over simplifying, some edge cases exist).  2. Read it into memory with NtOpenFile and NtReadFile (read-only).  This can be a little tricky, depending on where your payload is executing, but you can abuse some Windows components to access these files without a UAC prompt.  ICMLuaUtil!AllowAccessToTheWorld is an undocumented method. I have discussed it in the past (no one else has, no idea why), and using this method you can make any directory "global" to all users on the machine. My hypothesis is that you can abuse ICMLuaUtil!AllowAccessToTheWorld to change the ACL of C:\Windows\System32\config\ to allow your payload to access SYSTEM (offline registry) without triggering UAC.  For case one, using NtLoadKey and using an offline registry hive for registry modification and AV/EDR evasion isn't a new concept. But basically, use ICMLuaUtil!AllowAccessToTheWorld to access SYSTEM offline registry hive, make any modifications using RegSetValue, then "flush" the hive back to disk with NtUnloadKey and restart the machine.  For case two, use ICMLuaUtil!AllowAccessToTheWorld to access SYSTEM and read the file into memory with NtOpenFile and NtReadFile. Once loaded into memory, manually parse the registry (REGF parsing) to query registry keys without using any Windows registry API calls. However, this would be limited to read-only procedures. Case two would be very sneaky and would bamboozle security products.

🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-04-02 18:01 KST
🟠 위협 점수: 8/10 (높음)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 공격자들은 **Software Supply Chain Attack**을 통해 Axios와 같은 널리 사용되는 오픈소스 패키지를 타겟팅하여 광범위한 침투를 시도하고 있습니다. 또한, **ClickFix** 기법을 활용한 사회공학적 공격과 **MaaS(Malware-as-a-Service)** 형태의 CrystalRAT 배포가 활발해지며 공격의 진입 장벽이 낮아지고 있습니다. 특히 AI 에이전트의 권한 오남용 및 취약점을 악용한 데이터 유출 사례가 증가하고 있어, 클라우드 및 AI 인프라에 대한 보안 가시성 확보가 시급합니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
- **STARDUST CHOLLIMA (Crowdstrike)**: Axios npm 패키지 공급망 공격을 통해 기술 인프라 침투 시도.
- **Kimsuky (AhnLab ASEC)**: 악성 LNK 파일을 활용하여 파이썬 기반 백도어를 유포하는 공격 방식의 구조적 변화 확인.
- **TeamPCP (Unit42)**: 보안 인프라를 무기화하는 다단계 공급망 공격 수행 및 Vect 랜섬웨어 그룹과 파트너십 체결.
- **TA416 (CyberScoop)**: 유럽-중국 지정학적 이슈를 배경으로 한 사이버 스파이 활동 재개.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
- **KOSA-AAIBI MoU**: 한국-인도네시아 AI 산업 협력 체결 (데일리시큐).
- **Kimsuky 그룹 활동**: 국내 기업 및 기관을 대상으로 한 악성 LNK 파일 유포 방식 변화 감지 (AhnLab ASEC).
- **랜섬웨어 피해**: NetRunner 그룹이 대한민국 자동차 부품 제조 기업의 인도 법인을 대상으로 랜섬웨어 공격 수행 (AhnLab ASEC).

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
- **CVE-2026-1579**: PX4 Autopilot, CVSS 9.8, 드론 제어권 탈취 가능, 악용 여부 확인 중.
- **CVE-2026-20093**: Cisco IMC, CVSS 9.8, 인증 우회 취약점, 패치 권고.
- **CVE-2026-20160**: Cisco SSM On-Prem, CVSS 9.8, 원격 코드 실행(RCE), 악용 중.
- **CVE-2026-30643**: DedeCMS 5.7.118, CVSS 9.8, 원격 코드 실행(RCE).
- **CVE-2025-53521**: F5 BIG-IP APM, CVSS 9.8, 원격 코드 실행(RCE), 야생에서 활발히 악용 중.

### 5. IOC 요약
- **CVE**:
    - CVE-2026-20929 ([Crowdstrike](https://www.crowdstrike.com/en-us/blog/detecting-kerberos-relay-attack-via-dns-cname-abuse/))
    - CVE-2023-32434, CVE-2023-38606 ([Securelist](https://securelist.com/coruna-framework-updated-operation-triangulation-exploit/119228/))
    - CVE-2026-4681 ([CISA](https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/ics-advisories/icsa-26-085-03))
- **도메인/해시**: 이번 수집 데이터 내 직접적인 도메인 및 파일 해시 값은 명시되지 않음.

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
- **공급망 점검**: 프로젝트 내 사용 중인 Axios 패키지 버전을 확인하고, 최근 공급망 공격 관련 보안 권고 사항을 준수하여 의존성 업데이트 수행.
- **F5 BIG-IP APM 패치**: CVE-2025-53521 취약점에 노출된 14,000여 개 인스턴스에 대한 즉각적인 패치 및 웹쉘 존재 여부 포렌식 조사.
- **Cisco 장비 보안 강화**: Cisco IMC 및 SSM On-Prem 관련 인증 우회 및 RCE 취약점(CVE-2026-20093, CVE-2026-20160)에 대한 최신 펌웨어 업데이트 적용.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
- **점수**: 8/10
- **근거**: 공급망 공격(Axios)과 F5 BIG-IP APM 취약점 악용이 전 세계적으로 활발히 진행 중이며, 다수의 Critical 등급 취약점이 공개되어 즉각적인 대응이 필요한 상황임.
